summary: Test suite provides solid coverage of core domain logic (Secret model, report generation, output formatting) and workflow structure, but has significant gaps: (1) **Error handling is untested** — the implementation explicitly catches FileNotFoundError, JSONDecodeError, KeyError, ValueError, and date formatting errors, but none of these error paths are tested. (2) **CLI interface is not directly tested** — main() function, --reference-date, --warning-window, --format flags are never tested in isolation; they're only tested indirectly through act integration. (3) **Limited edge case variety** — no tests for invalid date formats, negative/zero rotation policies, malformed configs, empty secret names. (4) **Design could be more concise** — tests use hard-coded dates throughout, lack parametrization despite repetitive cases, and don't leverage pytest fixtures effectively. Strengths: Clear organization (unit vs integration), good boundary condition testing (window edges, expiry timing), comprehensive act harness that exercises the full CI/CD pipeline, readable test names. Would catch regressions in core business logic but would miss error handling regressions and CLI argument handling edge cases.
